items.title: Rabbits running rampant: Australia overrun by invasive species
items.description: In much of the world, rabbits are seen as harmless, fluffy pets. But in Australia, they’re an economic and environmental disaster. Introduced for sport hunting by British settlers in the mid-19th century, rabbits faced no natural predators. As a result, their population exploded across the continent within decades. Despite mass eradication campaigns, hundreds of millions of rabbits remain today, devastating the landscape and tormenting Australian farmers. FRANCE 24’s Grégory Plesse and Quentin Théron report.

items.title: Marc Bloch: 'Extraordinarily committed to projecting forward' history
items.description: As historian and WWII resistance fighter Marc Bloch is inducted into the Pantheon, Sharon Gaffney is pleased to welcome Dr. Andrew Smith, Historian of Modern France and Lecturer in Liberal Arts at Queen Mary University of London. Marc Bloch's entry into the Panthéon is described by Smith not simply as the commemoration of a distinguished historian, but as a profound statement about the contemporary crisis of democracy, truth, and citizenship. Smith argues that Bloch's enduring significance lies in the fusion of scholarship and civic responsibility: he was a historian who viewed the past as a tool for understanding the present, a citizen who defended republican values in times of national collapse, and a resistance fighter who ultimately sacrificed his life for those principles. In Smith's analysis, Bloch's "pantheonisation" arrives at a moment when France and other democracies confront challenges strikingly reminiscent of those Bloch diagnosed in the twentieth century: distrust of news and information, political polarisation, attacks on minorities, democratic fragility, and the erosion of truth. The ceremony therefore functions simultaneously as a tribute to a beloved intellectual and as a warning from history itself. Bloch emerges not merely as a figure of memory, but as a guide for navigating the democratic uncertainties of the present.

items.title: 'Climate adaptation increasingly means redesigning infrastructure for a hotter climate'
items.description: As Europe reels over a severe heatwave and the changing nature of climate change, Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Armel Castellan, Extreme Heat Services Technical Advisor at the World Meteorological Organization (WMO). Castellan suggests that extreme heat should no longer be understood as a temporary weather anomaly but as a structural public health, infrastructure, and societal challenge. His most striking observation is that today's heat extremes are effectively the baseline from which even hotter futures will emerge. He reframes heatwaves not simply as episodes of discomfort but as cascading crises that affect human health, energy systems, housing, agriculture, water security, and emergency services simultaneously.

items.title: France's Deschamps to miss Norway match to attend mother's funeral
items.description: France ⁠manager ⁠Didier Deschamps will ⁠not manage ​his team's final group-stage match against Norway on Friday as he ​flies back to France to attend his mother's funeral. France 24's Selina Sykes has the latest.

items.title: UN warns of worsening Ebola outbreak and El Niño-driven hunger threat
items.description: Officials from the UN and international aid agencies gathered in Geneva on Monday to discuss increasing threats of heatwaves in Europe, an intense Ebola outbreak in Africa, and an anticipated food crisis driven by weather events such as El Niño.

items.title: ICAC told favoured candidates got questions before uni's job interviews
items.description: A University of Wollongong governance manager has admitted that recruitment processes that led to her appointment were not fair or transparent.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: US: Google's YouTube settles teen mental health lawsuit
items.description: A Florida teenager said YouTube's addictive design contributed to depression, anxiety and sleep loss. Instagram, Snapchat and TikTok are also facing similar allegations about their impact on young users.

items.title: France reports Ebola case in doctor returning from Congo
items.description: A doctor who recently returned ​to France from a humanitarian mission in the Democratic Republic of Congo has tested positive for Ebola, marking the country's ​first confirmed ‌case linked to the current ⁠outbreak, the health ministry said on Wednesday.

items.title: UN maritime body starts enacting plan to move sailors out of Gulf
items.description: An evacuation plan to enable hundreds of ships to sail through the Strait of Hormuz is being enacted, according to the UN's International Maritime Organization.

items.title: Cerebras sinks 14% as full-year margin forecast disappoints
Algeria file complaint over referee's decision on Messi tackle
items.description: Argentina's skipper caught Algeria defender Aissa Mandi on the calf with a studs-up challenge from behind in the 30th minute of Tuesday's match but was not even booked. Leer más

items.title: Nigeria names six persons, three entities sanctioned for terrorism financing
items.description: The Nigerian sanction list includes a Lagos-based BDC operator and two companies - Nine to Nine BDC and Generation BDC Limited - named in the US designations on Tuesday. The post Nigeria names six persons, three entities sanctioned for terrorism financing appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Rubio arrives in UAE with aim to head off Gulf Arab unease over tentative Iran deal
items.description: Mr. Rubio arrived in Abu Dhabi late on Tuesday (June 23, 2026) following a two-day flurry of diplomatic activity between the U.S. and Iran in Switzerland led by Vice President J.D. Vance

items.title: Downing streak: On the Starmer resignation, British politics
items.description: Labour needs a progressive makeover to survive the far-right onslaught

items.title: 'VAR went for coffee' as England escape punishment against Ghana
items.description: The Black Stars' head coach Carlos Queiroz had strong words about the refereeing after two fouls from the Three Lions went unchecked, and the two sides played out a goalless draw. Meanwhile, Colombia qualified for the knockout round beating DR Congo 1-0 and Cristiano Ronaldo became the first player to score in six World Cups.

items.title: Arab League confirms Egyptian diplomat Nabil Fahmy as secretary general
items.description: The Arab League on Monday confirmed Egyptian veteran diplomat Nabil Fahmy as its new chief during a meeting of foreign ministers in Amman, a statement said.

items.title: Israeli attacks kill 2 in southern Lebanon despite ongoing Washington talks
items.description: Israeli defence minister says troops won’t withdraw from southern Lebanon as attacks slow but do not stop.

items.title: Iranians cautiously optimistic about thorny deal with US
items.description: Iran is watching closely as months of complex negotiations lie ahead amid internal political disputes.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV expected to visit Argentina in November on three-day stop
items.description: Argentine military bishop who met with pontiff at the Vatican this week says the itinerary is "practically defined" – though the Holy See has yet to issue an official announcement. Leer más

items.title: Watch: HL: Lucknow building fire: FIR cites illegal commercial use | Above the Fold | 23.06.2026
items.description: In this episode, we look at the devastating Lucknow building fire and the broader questions it raises about fire safety in India’s cities. We also examine the Centre’s latest FCRA amendments, Meta’s elevation of CRED founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp, and more.

items.title: Zohran Mamdani-backed candidates excel in congressional primaries
items.description: Zohran Mamdani’s slate of candidates with ties to the Democratic Socialists of America swept out area ​incumbent Democrats in a stunning win for the 34-year-old mayor

items.title: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba sues US government over defence blacklist
items.description: It is suing the US defence department after it was added to a list of firms with ties to the Chinese military.

items.title: Skipper Lionel Messi on Austria win: ‘It was very even, very intense’
items.description: Argentina’s captain scores both goals in 2-0 victory over Austria; “We’ve already given the fans plenty to cheer about, but we’ll try to give them one more reason to celebrate," says skipper. Leer más

items.title: Germany's pension plans draw praise and outrage
items.description: A 33-point plan to reform Germany's pension system has drawn praise and criticism in almost equal measure. Centrist politicians have called it a good compromise, but opposition parties on the left and right are unhappy.

items.title: Tech billionaire accused of assaulting partner, cutting $66k Birkin bag with scissors
items.description: A billionaire tech investor accused of assaulting his former partner and stealing expensive gifts he had bought her has his bail varied so he can visit his mother in Sydney.

items.title: France confirms first ever case of Ebola in doctor returning from DRC
items.description: France on Wednesday announced its first confirmed case of Ebola identified on its territory, a doctor back from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, which is fighting a major outbreak.

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: June 12 to 19
items.description: A selection of the stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Southern Lebanon's displaced Shiites fear return of old divisions
items.description: Israel has ordered Shiite-majority communities to be emptied, accusing residents of supporting Hezbollah, while allowing Christian, Sunni and other residents to remain. That risks aggravating sectarian tensions that have been mostly dormant since Lebanon's 1982-2000 civil war.

items.title: IMO, Oman announces plan to evacuate ships, 11,000 seafarers stranded in Persian Gulf
items.description: IMO says it has secured necessary safety guarantees, verified the conditions for safe navigation; operation involves cooperation with U.S., Iran, Oman, other coastal States in the region

items.title: Is Ronaldinho coming out of retirement?
items.description: Could a legendary #football player be coming out of #retirement? Former #Ballond'Or and #WorldCup winner #Ronaldinho has just signed with #Ravenna, a third-division Italian club.

items.title: Gina Rinehart names veterans' accommodation after Ben Roberts-Smith
items.description: Australia's richest person has opened a facility for homeless veterans in a beachside Perth suburb.

items.title: Malaysia central bank fines AEON Credit for sanctions breaches
ASIO warns of 'unprecedented threats' and defends pre-Bondi resourcing
items.description: Australia's domestic spy chief, Mike Burgess, has defended the agency's resourcing decisions in the years leading up to the Bondi terror attack, arguing his agency is grappling with an "unprecedented" volume of threats.

items.title: Milei to stand by his man as calls for Adorni’s sacking grow
items.description: Former ally Ramiro Marra becomes latest voice to call for official’s resignation; Mounting problems for top official, who admitted he failed to declare cash savings and is struggling to explain his expenditure. Leer más

items.title: US advisor expresses 'cautious optimism' on Libya after talks with Egyptian FM
items.description: Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty met with Senior Advisor to the US President for Arab and African Affairs, Massad Boulos, in Cairo on Sunday to discuss the situation in Libya.

items.title: Trump ally wins Colombia election, heralding pro-market turn
items.description: Election of conservative lawyer heralds swing back to business-friendly and pro-US policies after four years of leftist rule under Gustavo Petro. Leer más

items.title: Murray-Darling Basin communities call for balance in review
items.description: Communities across the Murray-Darling Basin are calling for a better balance, as irrigators say water buybacks are hurting their communities.

items.title: Senate passes state police bill, empowers governors to appoint police commissioners, give directives
items.description: The legislation seeks to establish a state policing structure that would operate concurrently with the existing federal police system. The post Senate passes state police bill, empowers governors to appoint police commissioners, give directives appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Sir Keir Starmer resigns as UK PM, Andy Burnham favourite to take over
items.description: Andy Burnham, who secured a return to Parliament just last week, appeared poised to succeed Starmer as Labour leader and UK PM after would-be rival Wes Streeting offered him his support. Leer más

items.title: Thanking family from ‘Kampala to Delhi’, Zohran Mamdani vows to govern ‘expansively, audaciously’ in inaugural speech as NYC Mayor
items.description: Mamdani inherits a city on the upswing, after years of slow recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic

items.title: UN nuclear chief says inspectors will visit Iran sites as part of war deal
items.description: Rafael Grossi says the IAEA is "working on modalities" but an Iranian minister says such access would only be part of a final deal with the US.
